chapters.en: And Jesus answering said, O faithless and perverse generation, how long shall I be with you, and suffer you? Bring thy son hither.

chapters.zh: 正来的时候，鬼把他摔倒，叫他重重的抽疯。耶稣就斥责那污鬼，把孩子治好了，交给他父亲。
chapters.en: And as he was yet a coming, the devil threw him down, and tare . And Jesus rebuked the unclean spirit, and healed the child, and delivered him again to his father.

chapters.zh: 众人都诧异神的大能（大能：或作威荣）。耶稣所作的一切事，众人正希奇的时候，耶稣对门徒说：
chapters.en: And they were all amazed at the mighty power of God. But while they wondered every one at all things which Jesus did, he said unto his disciples,

chapters.zh: 你们要把这些话存在耳中，因为人子将要被交在人手里。
chapters.en: Let these sayings sink down into your ears: for the Son of man shall be delivered into the hands of men.

chapters.zh: 他们不明白这话，意思乃是隐藏的，叫他们不能明白，他们也不敢问这话的意思。
chapters.en: But they understood not this saying, and it was hid from them, that they perceived it not: and they feared to ask him of that saying.

chapters.zh: 门徒中间起了议论，谁将为大。
chapters.en: Then there arose a reasoning among them, which of them should be greatest.

chapters.zh: 耶稣看出他们心中的议论，就领一个小孩子来，叫他站在自己旁边，
chapters.en: And Jesus, perceiving the thought of their heart, took a child, and set him by him,

chapters.zh: 对他们说：凡为我名接待这小孩子的，就是接待我；凡接待我的，就是接待那差我来的。你们中间最小的，他便为大。
chapters.en: And said unto them, Whosoever shall receive this child in my name receiveth me: and whosoever shall receive me receiveth him that sent me: for he that is least among you all, the same shall be great.

chapters.zh: 约翰说：夫子，我们看见一个人奉你的名赶鬼，我们就禁止他，因为他不与我们一同跟从你。
chapters.en: And John answered and said, Master, we saw one casting out devils in thy name; and we forbad him, because he followeth not with us.

chapters.zh: 耶稣说：不要禁止他；因为不敌挡你们的，就是帮助你们的。
chapters.en: And Jesus said unto him, Forbid not: for he that is not against us is for us.

chapters.zh: 耶稣被接上升的日子将到，他就定意向耶路撒冷去，
chapters.en: And it came to pass, when the time was come that he should be received up, he stedfastly set his face to go to Jerusalem,

chapters.zh: 便打发使者在他前头走。他们到了撒玛利亚的一个村庄，要为他预备。
chapters.en: And sent messengers before his face: and they went, and entered into a village of the Samaritans, to make ready for him.

chapters.zh: 那里的人不接待他，因他面向耶路撒冷去。
chapters.en: And they did not receive him, because his face was as though he would go to Jerusalem.

chapters.zh: 他的门徒雅各、约翰看见了，就说：主阿，你要我们吩咐火从天上降下来烧灭他们，像以利亚所作的（有古卷无「像以利亚所作的」数字）么？
chapters.en: And when his disciples James and John saw , they said, Lord, wilt thou that we command fire to come down from heaven, and consume them, even as Elias did?

chapters.zh: 耶稣转身责备两个门徒，说：你们的心如何，你们并不知道。
chapters.en: But he turned, and rebuked them, and said, Ye know not what manner of spirit ye are of.

chapters.zh: 人子来不是要灭人的性命（或作：灵魂；下同），是要救人的性命。说著就往别的村庄去了（有古卷只有五十五节首句，五十六节末句）。
chapters.en: For the Son of man is not come to destroy men's lives, but to save . And they went to another village.

chapters.zh: 他们走路的时候，有一人对耶稣说：你无论往那里去，我要跟从你。
chapters.en: And it came to pass, that, as they went in the way, a certain said unto him, Lord, I will follow thee whithersoever thou goest.

chapters.zh: 耶稣说：狐狸有洞，天空的飞鸟有窝，只是人子没有枕头的地方。
chapters.en: And Jesus said unto him, Foxes have holes, and birds of the air nests; but the Son of man hath not where to lay head.

chapters.zh: 又对一个人说：跟从我来！那人说：主，容我先回去埋葬我的父亲。
chapters.en: And he said unto another, Follow me. But he said, Lord, suffer me first to go and bury my father.

chapters.zh: 耶稣说：任凭死人埋葬他们的死人，你只管去传扬神国的道。
chapters.en: Jesus said unto him, Let the dead bury their dead: but go thou and preach the kingdom of God.

chapters.zh: 又有一人说：主，我要跟从你，但容我先去辞别我家里的人。
chapters.en: And another also said, Lord, I will follow thee; but let me first go bid them farewell, which are at home at my house.

chapters.zh: 耶稣说：手扶著犁向后看的，不配进神的国。
chapters.en: And Jesus said unto him, No man, having put his hand to the plough, and looking back, is fit for the kingdom of God.

chapters.zh: 这事以后，主又设立七十个人，差遣他们两个两个的在他前面，往自己所要到的各城各地方去，
chapters.en: After these things the Lord appointed other seventy also, and sent them two and two before his face into every city and place, whither he himself would come.

chapters.zh: 就对他们说：要收的庄稼多，作工的人少。所以，你们当求庄稼的主打发工人出去收他的庄稼。
chapters.en: Therefore said he unto them, The harvest truly great, but the labourers few: pray ye therefore the Lord of the harvest, that he would send forth labourers into his harvest.

chapters.zh: 你们去罢！我差你们出去，如同羊羔进入狼群。
chapters.en: Go your ways: behold, I send you forth as lambs among wolves.

chapters.zh: 不要带钱囊，不要带口袋，不要带鞋；在路上也不要问人的安。
chapters.en: Carry neither purse, nor scrip, nor shoes: and salute no man by the way.

chapters.zh: 无论进那一家，先要说：愿这一家平安。
chapters.en: And into whatsoever house ye enter, first say, Peace to this house.

chapters.zh: 那里若有当得平安的人（当得平安的人：原文是平安之子），你们所求的平安就必临到那家；不然，就归与你们了。
chapters.en: And if the son of peace be there, your peace shall rest upon it: if not, it shall turn to you again.

chapters.zh: 你们要住在那家，吃喝他们所供给的，因为工人得工价是应当的；不要从这家搬到那家。
chapters.en: And in the same house remain, eating and drinking such things as they give: for the labourer is worthy of his hire. Go not from house to house.

chapters.zh: 无论进那一城，人若接待你们，给你们摆上甚么，你们就吃甚么。
chapters.en: And into whatsoever city ye enter, and they receive you, eat such things as are set before you:

chapters.zh: 要医治那城里的病人，对他们说：神的国临近你们了。
chapters.en: And heal the sick that are therein, and say unto them, The kingdom of God is come nigh unto you.

chapters.zh: 无论进那一城，人若不接待你们，你们就到街上去，
chapters.en: But into whatsoever city ye enter, and they receive you not, go your ways out into the streets of the same, and say,

chapters.zh: 说：就是你们城里的尘土黏在我们的脚上，我们也当著你们擦去。虽然如此，你们该知道神的国临近了。
chapters.en: Even the very dust of your city, which cleaveth on us, we do wipe off against you: notwithstanding be ye sure of this, that the kingdom of God is come nigh unto you.

chapters.zh: 我告诉你们，当审判的日子，所多玛所受的，比那城还容易受呢！
chapters.en: But I say unto you, that it shall be more tolerable in that day for Sodom, than for that city.

chapters.zh: 哥拉汛哪，你有祸了！伯赛大阿，你有祸了！因为在你们中间所行的异能若行在推罗、西顿，他们早已披麻蒙灰，坐在地上悔改了。
chapters.en: Woe unto thee, Chorazin! woe unto thee, Bethsaida! for if the mighty works had been done in Tyre and Sidon, which have been done in you, they had a great while ago repented, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.

chapters.zh: 当审判的日子，推罗、西顿所受的，比你们还容易受呢！
chapters.en: But it shall be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon at the judgment, than for you.

chapters.zh: 迦百农阿，你已经升到天上（或作：你将要升到天上么），将来必推下阴间。
chapters.en: And thou, Capernaum, which art exalted to heaven, shalt be thrust down to hell.

chapters.zh: 又对门徒说：听从你们的就是听从我；弃绝你们的就是弃绝我；弃绝我的就是弃绝那差我来的。
chapters.en: He that heareth you heareth me; and he that despiseth you despiseth me; and he that despiseth me despiseth him that sent me.

chapters.zh: 那七十个人欢欢喜喜的回来，说：主阿！因你的名，就是鬼也服了我们。
chapters.en: And the seventy returned again with joy, saying, Lord, even the devils are subject unto us through thy name.

chapters.zh: 耶稣对他们说：我曾看见撒但从天上坠落，像闪电一样。
chapters.en: And he said unto them, I beheld Satan as lightning fall from heaven.

chapters.zh: 我已经给你们权柄可以践踏蛇和蝎子，又胜过仇敌一切的能力，断没有甚么能害你们。
chapters.en: Behold, I give unto you power to tread on serpents and scorpions, and over all the power of the enemy: and nothing shall by any means hurt you.

chapters.zh: 然而，不要因鬼服了你们就欢喜，要因你们的名记录在天上欢喜。
chapters.en: Notwithstanding in this rejoice not, that the spirits are subject unto you; but rather rejoice, because your names are written in heaven.

chapters.zh: 正当那时，耶稣被圣灵感动就欢乐，说：父阿，天地的主，我感谢你！因为你将这些事向聪明通达人就藏起来，向婴孩就显出来。父阿！是的，因为你的美意本是如此。
chapters.en: In that hour Jesus rejoiced in spirit, and said, I thank thee, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that thou hast hid these things from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed them unto babes: even so, Father; for so it seemed good in thy sight.

chapters.zh: 一切所有的都是我父交付我的；除了父，没有人知道子是谁；除了子和子所愿意指示的，没有人知道父是谁。
chapters.en: All things are delivered to me of my Father: and no man knoweth who the Son is, but the Father; and who the Father is, but the Son, and to whom the Son will reveal .

chapters.zh: 耶稣转身暗暗的对门徒说：看见你们所看见的，那眼睛就有福了。
chapters.en: And he turned him unto disciples, and said privately, Blessed the eyes which see the things that ye see:

chapters.zh: 我告诉你们，从前有许多先知和君王要看你们所看的，却没有看见，要听你们所听的，却没有听见。
chapters.en: For I tell you, that many prophets and kings have desired to see those things which ye see, and have not seen ; and to hear those things which ye hear, and have not heard .

chapters.zh: 有一个律法师起来试探耶稣，说：夫子！我该作甚么才可以承受永生？
chapters.en: And, behold, a certain lawyer stood up, and tempted him, saying, Master, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?

chapters.zh: 耶稣对他说：律法上写的是甚么？你念的是怎样呢？
chapters.en: He said unto him, What is written in the law? how readest thou?

chapters.zh: 他回答说：「你要尽心、尽性、尽力、尽意爱主 ─ 你的神；又要爱邻舍如同自己。」
chapters.en: And he answering said, Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy strength, and with all thy mind; and thy neighbour as thyself.

chapters.zh: 耶稣说：「你回答的是；你这样行，就必得永生。」
chapters.en: And he said unto him, Thou hast answered right: this do, and thou shalt live.

chapters.zh: 那人要显明自己有理，就对耶稣说：谁是我的邻舍呢？
chapters.en: But he, willing to justify himself, said unto Jesus, And who is my neighbour?

chapters.zh: 耶稣回答说：有一个人从耶路撒冷下耶利哥去，落在强盗手中。他们剥去他的衣裳，把他打个半死，就丢下他走了。
chapters.en: And Jesus answering said, A certain went down from Jerusalem to Jericho, and fell among thieves, which stripped him of his raiment, and wounded , and departed, leaving half dead.

chapters.zh: 偶然有一个祭司从这条路下来，看见他就从那边过去了。
chapters.en: And by chance there came down a certain priest that way: and when he saw him, he passed by on the other side.

chapters.zh: 又有一个利未人来到这地方，看见他，也照样从那边过去了。
chapters.en: And likewise a Levite, when he was at the place, came and looked , and passed by on the other side.

chapters.zh: 惟有一个撒玛利亚人行路来到那里，看见他就动了慈心，
chapters.en: But a certain Samaritan, as he journeyed, came where he was: and when he saw him, he had compassion ,

chapters.zh: 上前用油和酒倒在他的伤处，包裹好了，扶他骑上自己的牲口，带到店里去照应他。
chapters.en: And went to , and bound up his wounds, pouring in oil and wine, and set him on his own beast, and brought him to an inn, and took care of him.

chapters.zh: 第二天拿出二钱银子来，交给店主，说：你且照应他；此外所费用的，我回来必还你。
chapters.en: And on the morrow when he departed, he took out two pence, and gave to the host, and said unto him, Take care of him; and whatsoever thou spendest more, when I come again, I will repay thee.

chapters.zh: 你想，这三个人那一个是落在强盗手中的邻舍呢？
chapters.en: Which now of these three, thinkest thou, was neighbour unto him that fell among the thieves?

chapters.zh: 他说：是怜悯他的。耶稣说：你去照样行罢。
chapters.en: And he said, He that shewed mercy on him. Then said Jesus unto him, Go, and do thou likewise.

chapters.zh: 他们走路的时候，耶稣进了一个村庄。有一个女人，名叫马大，接他到自己家里。
chapters.en: Now it came to pass, as they went, that he entered into a certain village: and a certain woman named Martha received him into her house.

chapters.zh: 他有一个妹子，名叫马利亚，在耶稣脚前坐著听他的道。
chapters.en: And she had a sister called Mary, which also sat at Jesus' feet, and heard his word.

chapters.zh: 马大伺候的事多，心里忙乱，就进前来，说：主阿，我的妹子留下我一个人伺候，你不在意么？请吩咐他来帮助我。
chapters.en: But Martha was cumbered about much serving, and came to him, and said, Lord, dost thou not care that my sister hath left me to serve alone? bid her therefore that she help me.

chapters.zh: 耶稣回答说：马大！马大！你为许多的事思虑烦扰，
chapters.en: And Jesus answered and said unto her, Martha, Martha, thou art careful and troubled about many things:

chapters.zh: 但是不可少的只有一件；马利亚已经选择那上好的福分，是不能夺去的。
chapters.en: But one thing is needful: and Mary hath chosen that good part, which shall not be taken away from her.

chapters.zh: 耶稣在一个地方祷告；祷告完了，有个门徒对他说：求主教导我们祷告，像约翰教导他的门徒。
chapters.en: And it came to pass, that, as he was praying in a certain place, when he ceased, one of his disciples said unto him, Lord, teach us to pray, as John also taught his disciples.

chapters.zh: 耶稣说：你们祷告的时候，要说：我们在天上的父（有古卷只作：父阿）：愿人都尊你的名为圣。愿你的国降临；愿你的旨意行在地上，如同行在天上（有古卷无愿你的旨意云云）。
chapters.en: And he said unto them, When ye pray, say, Our Father which art in heaven, Hallowed be thy name. Thy kingdom come. Thy will be done, as in heaven, so in earth.

chapters.zh: 我们日用的饮食，天天赐给我们。
chapters.en: Give us day by day our daily bread.

chapters.zh: 赦免我们的罪，因为我们也赦免凡亏欠我们的人。不叫我们遇见试探；救我们脱离凶恶（有古卷无末句）。
chapters.en: And forgive us our sins; for we also forgive every one that is indebted to us. And lead us not into temptation; but deliver us from evil.

chapters.zh: 耶稣又说：你们中间谁有一个朋友半夜到他那里去，说：朋友！请借给我三个饼；
chapters.en: And he said unto them, Which of you shall have a friend, and shall go unto him at midnight, and say unto him, Friend, lend me three loaves;

chapters.zh: 因为我有一个朋友行路，来到我这里，我没有甚么给他摆上。
chapters.en: For a friend of mine in his journey is come to me, and I have nothing to set before him?

chapters.zh: 那人在里面回答说：不要搅扰我，门已经关闭，孩子们也同我在床上了，我不能起来给你。
chapters.en: And he from within shall answer and say, Trouble me not: the door is now shut, and my children are with me in bed; I cannot rise and give thee.

chapters.zh: 我告诉你们，虽不因他是朋友起来给他，但因他情词迫切的直求，就必起来照他所需用的给他。
chapters.en: I say unto you, Though he will not rise and give him, because he is his friend, yet because of his importunity he will rise and give him as many as he needeth.

chapters.zh: 我又告诉你们，你们祈求，就给你们；寻找，就寻见；叩门，就给你们开门。
chapters.en: And I say unto you, Ask, and it shall be given you; seek, and ye shall find; knock, and it shall be opened unto you.

chapters.zh: 因为，凡祈求的，就得著；寻找的，就寻见；叩门的，就给他开门。
chapters.en: For every one that asketh receiveth; and he that seeketh findeth; and to him that knocketh it shall be opened.

chapters.zh: 你们中间作父亲的，谁有儿子求饼，反给他石头呢？求鱼，反拿蛇当鱼给他呢？
chapters.en: If a son shall ask bread of any of you that is a father, will he give him a stone? or if a fish, will he for a fish give him a serpent?

chapters.zh: 求鸡蛋，反给他蝎子呢？
chapters.en: Or if he shall ask an egg, will he offer him a scorpion?

chapters.zh: 你们虽然不好，尚且知道拿好东西给儿女；何况天父，岂不更将圣灵给求他的人么？
chapters.en: If ye then, being evil, know how to give good gifts unto your children: how much more shall heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to them that ask him?

chapters.zh: 耶稣赶出一个叫人哑吧的鬼，鬼出去了，哑吧就说出话来；众人都希奇。
chapters.en: And he was casting out a devil, and it was dumb. And it came to pass, when the devil was gone out, the dumb spake; and the people wondered.

chapters.zh: 内中却有人说：他是靠著鬼王别西卜赶鬼。
chapters.en: But some of them said, He casteth out devils through Beelzebub the chief of the devils.

chapters.zh: 又有人试探耶稣，向他求从天上来的神迹。
chapters.en: And others, tempting , sought of him a sign from heaven.

chapters.zh: 他晓得他们的意念，便对他们说：凡一国自相纷争，就成为荒场；凡一家自相纷争，就必败落。
chapters.en: But he, knowing their thoughts, said unto them, Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation; and a house against a house falleth.

chapters.zh: 若撒但自相纷争，他的国怎能站得住呢？因为你们说我是靠著别西卜赶鬼。
chapters.en: If Satan also be divided against himself, how shall his kingdom stand? because ye say that I cast out devils through Beelzebub.

chapters.zh: 我若靠著别西卜赶鬼，你们的子弟赶鬼又靠著谁呢？这样，他们就要断定你们的是非。
chapters.en: And if I by Beelzebub cast out devils, by whom do your sons cast out? therefore shall they be your judges.

chapters.zh: 我若靠著神的能力赶鬼，这就是神的国临到你们了。
chapters.en: But if I with the finger of God cast out devils, no doubt the kingdom of God is come upon you.

chapters.zh: 壮士披挂整齐，看守自己的住宅，他所有的都平安无事；
chapters.en: When a strong man armed keepeth his palace, his goods are in peace:

chapters.zh: 但有一个比他更壮的来，胜过他，就夺去他所倚靠的盔甲兵器，又分了他的赃。
chapters.en: But when a stronger than he shall come upon him, and overcome him, he taketh from him all his armour wherein he trusted, and divideth his spoils.

chapters.zh: 不与我相合的，就是敌我的；不同我收聚的，就是分散的。
chapters.en: He that is not with me is against me: and he that gathereth not with me scattereth.

chapters.zh: 污鬼离了人身，就在无水之地过来过去，寻求安歇之处；既寻不著，便说：我要回到我所出来的屋里去。
chapters.en: When the unclean spirit is gone out of a man, he walketh through dry places, seeking rest; and finding none, he saith, I will return unto my house whence I came out.

chapters.zh: 到了，就看见里面打扫干净，修饰好了，
chapters.en: And when he cometh, he findeth swept and garnished.

chapters.zh: 便去另带了七个比自己更恶的鬼来，都进去住在那里。那人末后的景况比先前更不好了。
chapters.en: Then goeth he, and taketh seven other spirits more wicked than himself; and they enter in, and dwell there: and the last of that man is worse than the first.

chapters.zh: 耶稣正说这话的时候，众人中间有一个女人大声说：怀你胎的和乳养你的有福了！
chapters.en: And it came to pass, as he spake these things, a certain woman of the company lifted up her voice, and said unto him, Blessed the womb that bare thee, and the paps which thou hast sucked.

chapters.zh: 耶稣说：是，却还不如听神之道而遵守的人有福。
chapters.en: But he said, Yea rather, blessed they that hear the word of God, and keep it.

chapters.zh: 当众人聚集的时候，耶稣开讲说：这世代是一个邪恶的世代。他们求看神迹，除了约拿的神迹以外，再没有神迹给他们看。
chapters.en: And when the people were gathered thick together, he began to say, This is an evil generation: they seek a sign; and there shall no sign be given it, but the sign of Jonas the prophet.

chapters.zh: 约拿怎样为尼尼微人成了神迹，人子也要照样为这世代的人成了神迹。
chapters.en: For as Jonas was a sign unto the Ninevites, so shall also the Son of man be to this generation.

chapters.zh: 当审判的时候，南方的女王要起来定这世代的罪；因为他从地极而来，要听所罗门的智慧话。看哪！在这里有一人比所罗门更大。
chapters.en: The queen of the south shall rise up in the judgment with the men of this generation, and condemn them: for she came from the utmost parts of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and, behold, a greater than Solomon here.

chapters.zh: 当审判的时候，尼尼微人要起来定这世代的罪，因为尼尼微人听了约拿所传的就悔改了。看哪！在这里有一人比约拿更大。
chapters.en: The men of Nineve shall rise up in the judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: for they repented at the preaching of Jonas; and, behold, a greater than Jonas here.

chapters.zh: 没有人点灯放在地窨子里，或是斗底下，总是放在灯台上，使进来的人得见亮光。
chapters.en: No man, when he hath lighted a candle, putteth in a secret place, neither under a bushel, but on a candlestick, that they which come in may see the light.

chapters.zh: 你眼睛就是身上的灯。你的眼睛若瞭亮，全身就光明；眼睛若昏花，全身就黑暗。
chapters.en: The light of the body is the eye: therefore when thine eye is single, thy whole body also is full of light; but when is evil, thy body also full of darkness.

chapters.zh: 所以，你要省察，恐怕你里头的光或者黑暗了。
chapters.en: Take heed therefore that the light which is in thee be not darkness.

chapters.zh: 若是你全身光明，毫无黑暗，就必全然光明，如同灯的明光照亮你。
chapters.en: If thy whole body therefore full of light, having no part dark, the whole shall be full of light, as when the bright shining of a candle doth give thee light.

chapters.zh: 说话的时候，有一个法利赛人请耶稣同他吃饭，耶稣就进去坐席。
chapters.en: And as he spake, a certain Pharisee besought him to dine with him: and he went in, and sat down to meat.

chapters.zh: 这法利赛人看见耶稣饭前不洗手便诧异。
chapters.en: And when the Pharisee saw , he marvelled that he had not first washed before dinner.

chapters.zh: 主对他说：如今你们法利赛人洗净杯盘的外面，你们里面却满了勒索和邪恶。
chapters.en: And the Lord said unto him, Now do ye Pharisees make clean the outside of the cup and the platter; but your inward part is full of ravening and wickedness.

chapters.zh: 无知的人哪，造外面的，不也造里面么？
chapters.en: fools, did not he that made that which is without make that which is within also?

chapters.zh: 只要把里面的施舍给人，凡物于你们就都洁净了。
chapters.en: But rather give alms of such things as ye have; and, behold, all things are clean unto you.

chapters.zh: 你们法利赛人有祸了！因为你们将薄荷、芸香并各样菜蔬献上十分之一，那公义和爱神的事反倒不行了。这原是你们当行的；那也是不可不行的。
chapters.en: But woe unto you, Pharisees! for ye tithe mint and rue and all manner of herbs, and pass over judgment and the love of God: these ought ye to have done, and not to leave the other undone.

chapters.zh: 你们法利赛人有祸了！因为你们喜爱会堂里的首位，又喜爱人在街市上问你们的安。
chapters.en: Woe unto you, Pharisees! for ye love the uppermost seats in the synagogues, and greetings in the markets.

chapters.zh: 你们有祸了！因为你们如同不显露的坟墓，走在上面的人并不知道。
chapters.en: Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye are as graves which appear not, and the men that walk over are not aware .

chapters.zh: 律法师中有一个回答耶稣说：夫子！你这样说也把我们糟蹋了。
chapters.en: Then answered one of the lawyers, and said unto him, Master, thus saying thou reproachest us also.

chapters.zh: 耶稣说：你们律法师也有祸了！因为你们把难担的担子放在人身上，自己一个指头却不肯动。
chapters.en: And he said, Woe unto you also, lawyers! for ye lade men with burdens grievous to be borne, and ye yourselves touch not the burdens with one of your fingers.

chapters.zh: 你们有祸了！因为你们修造先知的坟墓，那先知正是你们的祖宗所杀的。
chapters.en: Woe unto you! for ye build the sepulchres of the prophets, and your fathers killed them.

chapters.zh: 可见你们祖宗所作的事，你们又证明又喜欢；因为他们杀了先知，你们修造先知的坟墓。
chapters.en: Truly ye bear witness that ye allow the deeds of your fathers: for they indeed killed them, and ye build their sepulchres.

chapters.zh: 所以，神用智慧（用智慧：或作的智者）曾说：我要差遣先知和使徒到他们那里去，有的他们要杀害，有的他们要逼迫，
chapters.en: Therefore also said the wisdom of God, I will send them prophets and apostles, and of them they shall slay and persecute:

chapters.zh: 使创世以来所流众先知血的罪都要问在这世代的人身上，
chapters.en: That the blood of all the prophets, which was shed from the foundation of the world, may be required of this generation;

chapters.zh: 就是从亚伯的血起，直到被杀在坛和殿中间撒迦利亚的血为止。我实在告诉你们，这都要问在这世代的人身上。
chapters.en: From the blood of Abel unto the blood of Zacharias, which perished between the altar and the temple: verily I say unto you, It shall be required of this generation.

chapters.zh: 你们律法师有祸了！因为你们把知识的钥匙夺了去，自己不进去，正要进去的人你们也阻挡他们。
chapters.en: Woe unto you, lawyers! for ye have taken away the key of knowledge: ye entered not in yourselves, and them that were entering in ye hindered.

chapters.zh: 耶稣从那里出来，文士和法利赛人就极力的催逼他，引动他多说话，
chapters.en: And as he said these things unto them, the scribes and the Pharisees began to urge vehemently, and to provoke him to speak of many things:

chapters.zh: 私下窥听，要拿他的话柄。
chapters.en: Laying wait for him, and seeking to catch something out of his mouth, that they might accuse him.

chapters.zh: 这时，有几万人聚集，甚至彼此践踏。耶稣开讲，先对门徒说：你们要防备法利赛人的酵，就是假冒为善。
chapters.en: In the mean time, when there were gathered together an innumerable multitude of people, insomuch that they trode one upon another, he began to say unto his disciples first of all, Beware ye of the leaven of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy.

chapters.zh: 掩盖的事没有不露出来的；隐藏的事，没有不被人知道的。
chapters.en: For there is nothing covered, that shall not be revealed; neither hid, that shall not be known.

chapters.zh: 因此，你们在暗中所说的，将要在明处被人听见；在内室附耳所说的，将要在房上被人宣扬。
chapters.en: Therefore whatsoever ye have spoken in darkness shall be heard in the light; and that which ye have spoken in the ear in closets shall be proclaimed upon the housetops.

chapters.zh: 我的朋友，我对你们说，那杀身体以后不能再作甚么的，不要怕他们。
chapters.en: And I say unto you my friends, Be not afraid of them that kill the body, and after that have no more that they can do.

chapters.zh: 我要指示你们当怕的是谁：当怕那杀了以后又有权柄丢在地狱里的。我实在告诉你们，正要怕他。
chapters.en: But I will forewarn you whom ye shall fear: Fear him, which after he hath killed hath power to cast into hell; yea, I say unto you, Fear him.

chapters.zh: 五个麻雀不是卖二分银子么？但在神面前，一个也不忘记；
chapters.en: Are not five sparrows sold for two farthings, and not one of them is forgotten before God?

chapters.zh: 就是你们的头发，也都被数过了。不要惧怕，你们比许多麻雀还贵重！
chapters.en: But even the very hairs of your head are all numbered. Fear not therefore: ye are of more value than many sparrows.

chapters.zh: 我又告诉你们，凡在人面前认我的，人子在神的使者面前也必认他；
chapters.en: Also I say unto you, Whosoever shall confess me before men, him shall the Son of man also confess before the angels of God:

chapters.zh: 在人面前不认我的，人子在神的使者面前也必不认他。
chapters.en: But he that denieth me before men shall be denied before the angels of God.

chapters.zh: 凡说话干犯人子的，还可得赦免；惟独亵渎圣灵的，总不得赦免。
chapters.en: And whosoever shall speak a word against the Son of man, it shall be forgiven him: but unto him that blasphemeth against the Holy Ghost it shall not be forgiven.

chapters.zh: 人带你们到会堂，并官府和有权柄的人面前，不要思虑怎么分诉，说甚么话；
chapters.en: And when they bring you unto the synagogues, and magistrates, and powers, take ye no thought how or what thing ye shall answer, or what ye shall say:

chapters.zh: 因为正在那时候，圣灵要指教你们当说的话。
chapters.en: For the Holy Ghost shall teach you in the same hour what ye ought to say.

chapters.zh: 众人中有一个人对耶稣说：夫子！请你吩咐我的兄长和我分开家业。
chapters.en: And one of the company said unto him, Master, speak to my brother, that he divide the inheritance with me.

chapters.zh: 耶稣说：你这个人！谁立我作你们断事的官，给你们分家业呢？
chapters.en: And he said unto him, Man, who made me a judge or a divider over you?

chapters.zh: 于是对众人说：你们要谨慎自守，免去一切的贪心，因为人的生命不在乎家道丰富。
chapters.en: And he said unto them, Take heed, and beware of covetousness: for a man's life consisteth not in the abundance of the things which he possesseth.

chapters.zh: 就用比喻对他们说：有一个财主田产丰盛；
chapters.en: And he spake a parable unto them, saying, The ground of a certain rich man brought forth plentifully:

chapters.zh: 自己心里思想说：我的出产没有地方收藏，怎么办呢？
chapters.en: And he thought within himself, saying, What shall I do, because I have no room where to bestow my fruits?

chapters.zh: 又说：我要这么办：要把我的仓房拆了，另盖更大的，在那里好收藏我一切的粮食和财物，
chapters.en: And he said, This will I do: I will pull down my barns, and build greater; and there will I bestow all my fruits and my goods.

chapters.zh: 然后要对我的灵魂说：灵魂哪，你有许多财物积存，可作多年的费用，只管安安逸逸的吃喝快乐罢！
chapters.en: And I will say to my soul, Soul, thou hast much goods laid up for many years; take thine ease, eat, drink, be merry.

chapters.zh: 神却对他说：无知的人哪，今夜必要你的灵魂；你所预备的要归谁呢？
chapters.en: But God said unto him, fool, this night thy soul shall be required of thee: then whose shall those things be, which thou hast provided?

chapters.zh: 凡为自己积财，在神面前却不富足的，也是这样。
chapters.en: So he that layeth up treasure for himself, and is not rich toward God.

chapters.zh: 耶稣又对门徒说：所以我告诉你们，不要为生命忧虑吃甚么，为身体忧虑穿甚么；
chapters.en: And he said unto his disciples, Therefore I say unto you, Take no thought for your life, what ye shall eat; neither for the body, what ye shall put on.

chapters.zh: 因为生命胜于饮食，身体胜于衣裳。
chapters.en: The life is more than meat, and the body than raiment.

chapters.zh: 你想乌鸦，也不种也不收，又没有仓又没有库，神尚且养活他。你们比飞鸟是何等的贵重呢！
chapters.en: Consider the ravens: for they neither sow nor reap; which neither have storehouse nor barn; and God feedeth them: how much more are ye better than the fowls?

chapters.zh: 你们那一个能用思虑使寿数多加一刻呢（或作：使身量多加一肘呢）？
chapters.en: And which of you with taking thought can add to his stature one cubit?

chapters.zh: 这最小的事，你们尚且不能作，为甚么还忧虑其余的事呢？
chapters.en: If ye then be not able to do that thing which is least, why take ye thought for the rest?

chapters.zh: 你想百合花怎么长起来；他也不劳苦，也不纺线。然而我告诉你们，就是所罗门极荣华的时候，他所穿戴的，还不如这花一朵呢！
chapters.en: Consider the lilies how they grow: they toil not, they spin not; and yet I say unto you, that Solomon in all his glory was not arrayed like one of these.

chapters.zh: 你们这小信的人哪，野地里的草今天还在，明天就丢在炉里，神还给他这样的妆饰，何况你们呢！
chapters.en: If then God so clothe the grass, which is to day in the field, and to morrow is cast into the oven; how much more you, O ye of little faith?

chapters.zh: 你们不要求吃甚么，喝甚么，也不要罣心；
chapters.en: And seek not ye what ye shall eat, or what ye shall drink, neither be ye of doubtful mind.

chapters.zh: 这都是外邦人所求的。你们必须用这些东西，你们的父是知道的。
chapters.en: For all these things do the nations of the world seek after: and your Father knoweth that ye have need of these things.

chapters.zh: 你们只要求他的国，这些东西就必加给你们了。
chapters.en: But rather seek ye the kingdom of God; and all these things shall be added unto you.

chapters.zh: 你们这小群，不要惧怕，因为你们的父乐意把国赐给你们。
chapters.en: Fear not, little flock; for it is your Father's good pleasure to give you the kingdom.

chapters.zh: 你们要变卖所有的赒济人，为自己预备永不坏的钱囊，用不尽的财宝在天上，就是贼不能近、虫不能蛀的地方。
chapters.en: Sell that ye have, and give alms; provide yourselves bags which wax not old, a treasure in the heavens that faileth not, where no thief approacheth, neither moth corrupteth.

chapters.zh: 因为，你们的财宝在那里，你们的心也在那里。
chapters.en: For where your treasure is, there will your heart be also.

chapters.zh: 你们腰里要束上带，灯也要点著，
chapters.en: Let your loins be girded about, and lights burning;

chapters.zh: 自己好像仆人等候主人从婚姻的筵席上回来。他来到，叩门，就立刻给他开门。
chapters.en: And ye yourselves like unto men that wait for their lord, when he will return from the wedding; that when he cometh and knocketh, they may open unto him immediately.

chapters.zh: 主人来了，看见仆人儆醒，那仆人就有福了。我实在告诉你们，主人必叫他们坐席，自己束上带，进前伺候他们。
chapters.en: Blessed those servants, whom the lord when he cometh shall find watching: verily I say unto you, that he shall gird himself, and make them to sit down to meat, and will come forth and serve them.

chapters.zh: 或是二更天来，或是三更天来，看见仆人这样，那仆人就有福了。
chapters.en: And if he shall come in the second watch, or come in the third watch, and find so, blessed are those servants.

chapters.zh: 家主若知道贼甚么时候来，就必儆醒，不容贼挖透房屋，这是你们所知道的。
chapters.en: And this know, that if the goodman of the house had known what hour the thief would come, he would have watched, and not have suffered his house to be broken through.

chapters.zh: 你们也要预备；因为你们想不到的时候，人子就来了。
chapters.en: Be ye therefore ready also: for the Son of man cometh at an hour when ye think not.

chapters.zh: 彼得说：主阿，这比喻是为我们说的呢？还是为众人呢？
chapters.en: Then Peter said unto him, Lord, speakest thou this parable unto us, or even to all?

chapters.zh: 主说：谁是那忠心有见识的管家，主人派他管理家里的人，按时分粮给他们呢？
chapters.en: And the Lord said, Who then is that faithful and wise steward, whom lord shall make ruler over his household, to give portion of meat in due season?

chapters.zh: 主人来到，看见仆人这样行，那仆人就有福了。
chapters.en: Blessed that servant, whom his lord when he cometh shall find so doing.

chapters.zh: 我实在告诉你们，主人要派他管理一切所有的。
chapters.en: Of a truth I say unto you, that he will make him ruler over all that he hath.

chapters.zh: 那仆人若心里说：我的主人必来得迟，就动手打仆人和使女，并且吃喝醉酒；
chapters.en: But and if that servant say in his heart, My lord delayeth his coming; and shall begin to beat the menservants and maidens, and to eat and drink, and to be drunken;

chapters.zh: 在他想不到的日子，不知道的时辰，那仆人的主人要来，重重的处治他（或作：把他腰轧了），定他和不忠心的人同罪。
chapters.en: The lord of that servant will come in a day when he looketh not for , and at an hour when he is not aware, and will cut him in sunder, and will appoint him his portion with the unbelievers.

chapters.zh: 仆人知道主人的意思，却不预备，又不顺他的意思行，那仆人必多受责打；
chapters.en: And that servant, which knew his lord's will, and prepared not , neither did according to his will, shall be beaten with many .

chapters.zh: 惟有那不知道的，做了当受责打的事，必少受责打；因为多给谁，就向谁多取；多托谁，就向谁多要。
chapters.en: But he that knew not, and did commit things worthy of stripes, shall be beaten with few . For unto whomsoever much is given, of him shall be much required: and to whom men have committed much, of him they will ask the more.

chapters.zh: 我来要把火丢在地上，倘若已经著起来，不也是我所愿意的么？
chapters.en: I am come to send fire on the earth; and what will I, if it be already kindled?

chapters.zh: 我有当受的洗还没有成就，我是何等的迫切呢？
chapters.en: But I have a baptism to be baptized with; and how am I straitened till it be accomplished!

chapters.zh: 你们以为我来，是叫地上太平么？我告诉你们，不是，乃是叫人分争。
chapters.en: Suppose ye that I am come to give peace on earth? I tell you, Nay; but rather division:

chapters.zh: 从今以后，一家五个人将要分争：三个人和两个人相争，两个人和三个人相争；
chapters.en: For from henceforth there shall be five in one house divided, three against two, and two against three.

chapters.zh: 父亲和儿子相争，儿子和父亲相争；母亲和女儿相争，女儿和母亲相争；婆婆和媳妇相争，媳妇和婆婆相争。
chapters.en: The father shall be divided against the son, and the son against the father; the mother against the daughter, and the daughter against the mother; the mother in law against her daughter in law, and the daughter in law against her mother in law.

chapters.zh: 耶稣又对众人说：你们看见西边起了云彩，就说：要下一阵雨；果然就有。
chapters.en: And he said also to the people, When ye see a cloud rise out of the west, straightway ye say, There cometh a shower; and so it is.

chapters.zh: 起了南风，就说：将要燥热；也就有了。
chapters.en: And when the south wind blow, ye say, There will be heat; and it cometh to pass.

chapters.zh: 假冒为善的人哪，你们知道分辨天地的气色，怎么不知道分辨这时候呢？
chapters.en: hypocrites, ye can discern the face of the sky and of the earth; but how is it that ye do not discern this time?

chapters.zh: 你们又为何不自己审量甚么是合理的呢？
chapters.en: Yea, and why even of yourselves judge ye not what is right?

chapters.zh: 你同告你的对头去见官，还在路上，务要尽力的和他了结；恐怕他拉你到官面前，官交付差役，差役把你下在监里。
chapters.en: When thou goest with thine adversary to the magistrate, in the way, give diligence that thou mayest be delivered from him; lest he hale thee to the judge, and the judge deliver thee to the officer, and the officer cast thee into prison.

chapters.zh: 我告诉你，若有半文钱没有还清，你断不能从那里出来。
chapters.en: I tell thee, thou shalt not depart thence, till thou hast paid the very last mite.

chapters.zh: 正当那时，有人将彼拉多使加利利人的血搀杂在他们祭物中的事告诉耶稣。
chapters.en: There were present at that season some that told him of the Galilaeans, whose blood Pilate had mingled with their sacrifices.

chapters.zh: 耶稣说；你们以为这些加利利人比众加利利人更有罪，所以受这害么？
chapters.en: And Jesus answering said unto them, Suppose ye that these Galilaeans were sinners above all the Galilaeans, because they suffered such things?

chapters.zh: 我告诉你们，不是的！你们若不悔改，都要如此灭亡！
chapters.en: I tell you, Nay: but, except ye repent, ye shall all likewise perish.

chapters.zh: 从前西罗亚楼倒塌了，压死十八个人；你们以为那些人比一切住在耶路撒冷的人更有罪么？
chapters.en: Or those eighteen, upon whom the tower in Siloam fell, and slew them, think ye that they were sinners above all men that dwelt in Jerusalem?

chapters.zh: 我告诉你们，不是的！你们若不悔改，都要如此灭亡！
chapters.en: I tell you, Nay: but, except ye repent, ye shall all likewise perish.

chapters.zh: 于是用比喻说：一个人有一棵无花果树栽在葡萄园里。他来到树前找果子，却找不著。
chapters.en: He spake also this parable; A certain had a fig tree planted in his vineyard; and he came and sought fruit thereon, and found none.

chapters.zh: 就对管园的说：看哪，我这三年来到这无花果树前找果子，竟找不著。把他砍了罢，何必白占地土呢！
chapters.en: Then said he unto the dresser of his vineyard, Behold, these three years I come seeking fruit on this fig tree, and find none: cut it down; why cumbereth it the ground?

chapters.zh: 管园的说：主阿，今年且留著，等我周围掘开土，加上粪；
chapters.en: And he answering said unto him, Lord, let it alone this year also, till I shall dig about it, and dung :

chapters.zh: 以后若结果子便罢，不然再把他砍了。
chapters.en: And if it bear fruit, : and if not, after that thou shalt cut it down.

chapters.zh: 安息日，耶稣在会堂里教训人。
chapters.en: And he was teaching in one of the synagogues on the sabbath.

chapters.zh: 有一个女人被鬼附著，病了十八年，腰弯得一点直不起来。
chapters.en: And, behold, there was a woman which had a spirit of infirmity eighteen years, and was bowed together, and could in no wise lift up .

chapters.zh: 耶稣看见，便叫过他来，对他说：女人，你脱离这病了！
chapters.en: And when Jesus saw her, he called , and said unto her, Woman, thou art loosed from thine infirmity.

chapters.zh: 于是用两只手按著他；他立刻直起腰来，就归荣耀与神。
chapters.en: And he laid hands on her: and immediately she was made straight, and glorified God.

chapters.zh: 管会堂的因为耶稣在安息日治病，就气忿忿的对众人说：有六日应当做工；那六日之内可以来求医，在安息日却不可。
chapters.en: And the ruler of the synagogue answered with indignation, because that Jesus had healed on the sabbath day, and said unto the people, There are six days in which men ought to work: in them therefore come and be healed, and not on the sabbath day.

chapters.zh: 主说：假冒为善的人哪，难道你们各人在安息日不解开槽上的牛、驴，牵去饮么？
chapters.en: The Lord then answered him, and said, hypocrite, doth not each one of you on the sabbath loose his ox or ass from the stall, and lead away to watering?

chapters.zh: 况且这女人本是亚伯拉罕的后裔，被撒但捆绑了这十八年，不当在安息日解开他的绑么？
chapters.en: And ought not this woman, being a daughter of Abraham, whom Satan hath bound, lo, these eighteen years, be loosed from this bond on the sabbath day?

chapters.zh: 耶稣说这话，他的敌人都惭愧了；众人因他所行一切荣耀的事，就都欢喜了。
chapters.en: And when he had said these things, all his adversaries were ashamed: and all the people rejoiced for all the glorious things that were done by him.

chapters.zh: 耶稣说：神的国好像甚么？我拿甚么来比较呢？
chapters.en: Then said he, Unto what is the kingdom of God like? and whereunto shall I resemble it?

chapters.zh: 好像一粒芥菜种，有人拿去种在园子里，长大成树，天上的飞鸟宿在他的枝上。
chapters.en: It is like a grain of mustard seed, which a man took, and cast into his garden; and it grew, and waxed a great tree; and the fowls of the air lodged in the branches of it.

chapters.zh: 又说：我拿甚么来比神的国呢？
chapters.en: And again he said, Whereunto shall I liken the kingdom of God?

chapters.zh: 好比面酵，有妇人拿来藏在三斗面里，直等全团都发起来。
chapters.en: It is like leaven, which a woman took and hid in three measures of meal, till the whole was leavened.

chapters.zh: 耶稣往耶路撒冷去，在所经过的各城各乡教训人。
chapters.en: And he went through the cities and villages, teaching, and journeying toward Jerusalem.

chapters.zh: 有一个人问他说：主阿，得救的人少么？
chapters.en: Then said one unto him, Lord, are there few that be saved? And he said unto them,

chapters.zh: 耶稣对众人说：你们要努力进窄门。我告诉你们，将来有许多人想要进去，却是不能。
chapters.en: Strive to enter in at the strait gate: for many, I say unto you, will seek to enter in, and shall not be able.

chapters.zh: 及至家主起来关了门，你们站在外面叩门，说：主阿，给我们开门！他就回答说：我不认识你们，不晓得你们是那里来的！
chapters.en: When once the master of the house is risen up, and hath shut to the door, and ye begin to stand without, and to knock at the door, saying, Lord, Lord, open unto us; and he shall answer and say unto you, I know you not whence ye are:

chapters.zh: 那时，你们要说：我们在你面前吃过喝过，你也在我们的街上教训过人。
chapters.en: Then shall ye begin to say, We have eaten and drunk in thy presence, and thou hast taught in our streets.

chapters.zh: 他要说：我告诉你们，我不晓得你们是那里来的。你们这一切作恶的人，离开我去罢！
chapters.en: But he shall say, I tell you, I know you not whence ye are; depart from me, all workers of iniquity.

chapters.zh: 你们要看见亚伯拉罕、以撒、雅各，和众先知都在神的国里，你们却被赶到外面，在那里必要哀哭切齿了。
chapters.en: There shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth, when ye shall see Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob, and all the prophets, in the kingdom of God, and you thrust out.

chapters.zh: 从东、从西、从南、从北将有人来，在神的国里坐席。
chapters.en: And they shall come from the east, and the west, and from the north, and the south, and shall sit down in the kingdom of God.

chapters.zh: 只是有在后的，将要在前；有在前的，将要在后。
chapters.en: And, behold, there are last which shall be first, and there are first which shall be last.

chapters.zh: 正当那时，有几个法利赛人来对耶稣说：离开这里去罢，因为希律想要杀你。
chapters.en: The same day there came certain of the Pharisees, saying unto him, Get thee out, and depart hence: for Herod will kill thee.

chapters.zh: 耶稣说：你们去告诉那个狐狸说：今天、明天我赶鬼治病，第三天我的事就成全了。
chapters.en: And he said unto them, Go ye, and tell that fox, Behold, I cast out devils, and I do cures to day and to morrow, and the third I shall be perfected.

chapters.zh: 虽然这样，今天、明天、后天，我必须前行，因为先知在耶路撒冷之外丧命是不能的。
chapters.en: Nevertheless I must walk to day, and to morrow, and the following: for it cannot be that a prophet perish out of Jerusalem.

chapters.zh: 耶路撒冷阿！耶路撒冷阿！你常杀害先知，又用石头打死那奉差遣到你这里来的人。我多次愿意聚集你的儿女，好像母鸡把小鸡聚集在翅膀底下，只是你们不愿意。
chapters.en: O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, which killest the prophets, and stonest them that are sent unto thee; how often would I have gathered thy children together, as a hen her brood under wings, and ye would not!

chapters.zh: 看哪，你们的家成为荒场留给你们。我告诉你们，从今以后你们不得再见我，直等到你们说：奉主名来的是应当称颂的。
chapters.en: Behold, your house is left unto you desolate: and verily I say unto you, Ye shall not see me, until come when ye shall say, Blessed he that cometh in the name of the Lord.

chapters.zh: 安息日，耶稣到一个法利赛人的首领家里去吃饭，他们就窥探他。
chapters.en: And it came to pass, as he went into the house of one of the chief Pharisees to eat bread on the sabbath day, that they watched him.

chapters.zh: 在他面前有一个患水臌的人。
chapters.en: And, behold, there was a certain man before him which had the dropsy.

chapters.zh: 耶稣对律法师和法利赛人说：安息日治病，可以不可以？
chapters.en: And Jesus answering spake unto the lawyers and Pharisees, saying, Is it lawful to heal on the sabbath day?

chapters.zh: 他们却不言语。耶稣就治好那人，叫他走了；
chapters.en: And they held their peace. And he took , and healed him, and let him go;

chapters.zh: 便对他们说：你们中间谁有驴或有牛，在安息日掉在井里，不立时拉他上来呢？
chapters.en: And answered them, saying, Which of you shall have an ass or an ox fallen into a pit, and will not straightway pull him out on the sabbath day?

chapters.zh: 他们不能对答这话。
chapters.en: And they could not answer him again to these things.

chapters.zh: 耶稣见所请的客拣择首位，就用比喻对他们说：
chapters.en: And he put forth a parable to those which were bidden, when he marked how they chose out the chief rooms; saying unto them,

chapters.zh: 你被人请去赴婚姻的筵席，不要坐在首位上，恐怕有比你尊贵的客被他请来；
chapters.en: When thou art bidden of any to a wedding, sit not down in the highest room; lest a more honourable man than thou be bidden of him;

chapters.zh: 那请你们的人前来对你说：让座给这一位罢！你就羞羞惭惭的退到末位上去了。
chapters.en: And he that bade thee and him come and say to thee, Give this man place; and thou begin with shame to take the lowest room.

chapters.zh: 你被请的时候，就去坐在末位上，好叫那请你的人来对你说：朋友，请上坐。那时，你在同席的人面前就有光彩了。
chapters.en: But when thou art bidden, go and sit down in the lowest room; that when he that bade thee cometh, he may say unto thee, Friend, go up higher: then shalt thou have worship in the presence of them that sit at meat with thee.

chapters.zh: 因为，凡自高的，必降为卑，自卑的，必升为高。
chapters.en: For whosoever exalteth himself shall be abased; and he that humbleth himself shall be exalted.

chapters.zh: 耶稣又对请他的人说：你摆设午饭或晚饭，不要请你的朋友、弟兄、亲属，和富足的邻舍，恐怕他们也请你，你就得了报答。
chapters.en: Then said he also to him that bade him, When thou makest a dinner or a supper, call not thy friends, nor thy brethren, neither thy kinsmen, nor rich neighbours; lest they also bid thee again, and a recompence be made thee.

chapters.zh: 你摆设筵席，倒要请那贫穷的、残废的、瘸腿的、瞎眼的，你就有福了！
chapters.en: But when thou makest a feast, call the poor, the maimed, the lame, the blind:

chapters.zh: 因为他们没有甚么可报答你。到义人复活的时候，你要得著报答。
chapters.en: And thou shalt be blessed; for they cannot recompense thee: for thou shalt be recompensed at the resurrection of the just.

chapters.zh: 同席的有一人听见这话，就对耶稣说：在神国里吃饭的有福了！
chapters.en: And when one of them that sat at meat with him heard these things, he said unto him, Blessed he that shall eat bread in the kingdom of God.

chapters.zh: 耶稣对他说：有一人摆设大筵席，请了许多客。
chapters.en: Then said he unto him, A certain man made a great supper, and bade many:

chapters.zh: 到了坐席的时候，打发仆人去对所请的人说：请来罢！样样都齐备了。
chapters.en: And sent his servant at supper time to say to them that were bidden, Come; for all things are now ready.

chapters.zh: 众人一口同音的推辞。头一个说：我买了一块地，必须去看看。请你准我辞了。
chapters.en: And they all with one began to make excuse. The first said unto him, I have bought a piece of ground, and I must needs go and see it: I pray thee have me excused.

chapters.zh: 又有一个说：我买了五对牛，要去试一试。请你准我辞了。
chapters.en: And another said, I have bought five yoke of oxen, and I go to prove them: I pray thee have me excused.

chapters.zh: 又有一个说：我才娶了妻，所以不能去。
chapters.en: And another said, I have married a wife, and therefore I cannot come.

chapters.zh: 那仆人回来，把这事都告诉了主人。家主就动怒，对仆人说：快出去，到城里大街小巷，领那贫穷的、残废的、瞎眼的、瘸腿的来。
chapters.en: So that servant came, and shewed his lord these things. Then the master of the house being angry said to his servant, Go out quickly into the streets and lanes of the city, and bring in hither the poor, and the maimed, and the halt, and the blind.

chapters.zh: 仆人说：主阿，你所吩咐的已经办了，还有空座。
chapters.en: And the servant said, Lord, it is done as thou hast commanded, and yet there is room.

chapters.zh: 主人对仆人说，你出去到路上和篱笆那里，勉强人进来，坐满我的屋子。
chapters.en: And the lord said unto the servant, Go out into the highways and hedges, and compel to come in, that my house may be filled.

chapters.zh: 我告诉你们，先前所请的人，没有一个得尝我的筵席。
chapters.en: For I say unto you, That none of those men which were bidden shall taste of my supper.

chapters.zh: 有极多的人和耶稣同行。他转过来对他们说：
chapters.en: And there went great multitudes with him: and he turned, and said unto them,

chapters.zh: 人到我这里来，若不爱我胜过爱（爱我胜过爱：原文是恨）自己的父母、妻子、儿女、弟兄、姐妹，和自己的性命，就不能作我的门徒。
chapters.en: If any come to me, and hate not his father, and mother, and wife, and children, and brethren, and sisters, yea, and his own life also, he cannot be my disciple.

chapters.zh: 凡不背著自己十字架跟从我的，也不能作我的门徒。
chapters.en: And whosoever doth not bear his cross, and come after me, cannot be my disciple.

chapters.zh: 你们那一个要盖一座楼，不先坐下算计花费，能盖成不能呢？
chapters.en: For which of you, intending to build a tower, sitteth not down first, and counteth the cost, whether he have to finish ?

chapters.zh: 恐怕安了地基，不能成功，看见的人都笑话他，说：
chapters.en: Lest haply, after he hath laid the foundation, and is not able to finish , all that behold begin to mock him,

chapters.zh: 这个人开了工，却不能完工。
chapters.en: Saying, This man began to build, and was not able to finish.

chapters.zh: 或是一个王出去和别的王打仗，岂不先坐下酌量，能用一万兵去敌那领二万兵来攻打他的么？
chapters.en: Or what king, going to make war against another king, sitteth not down first, and consulteth whether he be able with ten thousand to meet him that cometh against him with twenty thousand?

chapters.zh: 若是不能，就趁敌人还远的时候，派使者去求和息的条款。
chapters.en: Or else, while the other is yet a great way off, he sendeth an ambassage, and desireth conditions of peace.

chapters.zh: 这样，你们无论甚么人，若不撇下一切所有的，就不能作我的门徒。
chapters.en: So likewise, whosoever he be of you that forsaketh not all that he hath, he cannot be my disciple.

chapters.zh: 盐本是好的；盐若失了味，可用甚么叫他再咸呢？
chapters.en: Salt good: but if the salt have lost his savour, wherewith shall it be seasoned?

chapters.zh: 或用在田里，或堆在粪里，都不合式，只好丢在外面。有耳可听的，就应当听！
chapters.en: It is neither fit for the land, nor yet for the dunghill; men cast it out. He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.

chapters.zh: 众税吏和罪人都挨近耶稣，要听他讲道。
chapters.en: Then drew near unto him all the publicans and sinners for to hear him.

chapters.zh: 法利赛人和文士私下议论说：这个人接待罪人，又同他们吃饭。
chapters.en: And the Pharisees and scribes murmured, saying, This man receiveth sinners, and eateth with them.

chapters.zh: 耶稣就用比喻说：
chapters.en: And he spake this parable unto them, saying,

chapters.zh: 你们中间谁有一百只羊失去一只，不把这九十九只撇在旷野、去找那失去的羊，直到找著呢？
chapters.en: What man of you, having an hundred sheep, if he lose one of them, doth not leave the ninety and nine in the wilderness, and go after that which is lost, until he find it?

chapters.zh: 找著了，就欢欢喜喜的扛在肩上，回到家里，
chapters.en: And when he hath found , he layeth on his shoulders, rejoicing.

chapters.zh: 就请朋友邻舍来，对他们说：我失去的羊已经找著了，你们和我一同欢喜罢！
chapters.en: And when he cometh home, he calleth together friends and neighbours, saying unto them, Rejoice with me; for I have found my sheep which was lost.

chapters.zh: 我告诉你们，一个罪人悔改，在天上也要这样为他欢喜，较比为九十九个不用悔改的义人欢喜更大。
chapters.en: I say unto you, that likewise joy shall be in heaven over one sinner that repenteth, more than over ninety and nine just persons, which need no repentance.

chapters.zh: 或是一个妇人有十块钱，若失落一块，岂不点上灯，打扫屋子，细细的找，直到找著么？
chapters.en: Either what woman having ten pieces of silver, if she lose one piece, doth not light a candle, and sweep the house, and seek diligently till she find ?

chapters.zh: 找著了，就请朋友邻舍来，对他们说：我失落的那块钱已经找著了，你们和我一同欢喜罢！
chapters.en: And when she hath found , she calleth friends and neighbours together, saying, Rejoice with me; for I have found the piece which I had lost.

chapters.zh: 我告诉你们，一个罪人悔改，在神的使者面前也是这样为他欢喜。」
chapters.en: Likewise, I say unto you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner that repenteth.

chapters.zh: 耶稣又说：一个人有两个儿子。
chapters.en: And he said, A certain man had two sons:

chapters.zh: 小儿子对父亲说：父亲，请你把我应得的家业分给我。他父亲就把产业分给他们。
chapters.en: And the younger of them said to father, Father, give me the portion of goods that falleth . And he divided unto them living.

chapters.zh: 过了不多几日，小儿子就把他一切所有的都收拾起来，往远方去了。在那里任意放荡，浪费赀财。
chapters.en: And not many days after the younger son gathered all together, and took his journey into a far country, and there wasted his substance with riotous living.

chapters.zh: 既耗尽了一切所有的，又遇著那地方大遭饥荒，就穷苦起来。
chapters.en: And when he had spent all, there arose a mighty famine in that land; and he began to be in want.

chapters.zh: 于是去投靠那地方的一个人；那人打发他到田里去放猪。
chapters.en: And he went and joined himself to a citizen of that country; and he sent him into his fields to feed swine.

chapters.zh: 他恨不得拿猪所吃的豆荚充饥，也没有人给他。
chapters.en: And he would fain have filled his belly with the husks that the swine did eat: and no man gave unto him.

chapters.zh: 他醒悟过来，就说：我父亲有多少的雇工，口粮有余，我倒在这里饿死么？
chapters.en: And when he came to himself, he said, How many hired servants of my father's have bread enough and to spare, and I perish with hunger!

chapters.zh: 我要起来，到我父亲那里去，向他说：父亲！我得罪了天，又得罪了你；
chapters.en: I will arise and go to my father, and will say unto him, Father, I have sinned against heaven, and before thee,

chapters.zh: 从今以后，我不配称为你的儿子，把我当作一个雇工罢！
chapters.en: And am no more worthy to be called thy son: make me as one of thy hired servants.

chapters.zh: 于是起来，往他父亲那里去。相离还远，他父亲看见，就动了慈心，跑去抱著他的颈项，连连与他亲嘴。
chapters.en: And he arose, and came to his father. But when he was yet a great way off, his father saw him, and had compassion, and ran, and fell on his neck, and kissed him.

chapters.zh: 儿子说：父亲！我得罪了天，又得罪了你；从今以后，我不配称为你的儿子。
chapters.en: And the son said unto him, Father, I have sinned against heaven, and in thy sight, and am no more worthy to be called thy son.

chapters.zh: 父亲却吩咐仆人说：把那上好的袍子快拿出来给他穿；把戒指戴在他指头上；把鞋穿在他脚上；
chapters.en: But the father said to his servants, Bring forth the best robe, and put on him; and put a ring on his hand, and shoes on feet:

chapters.zh: 把那肥牛犊牵来宰了，我们可以吃喝快乐；
chapters.en: And bring hither the fatted calf, and kill ; and let us eat, and be merry:

chapters.zh: 因为我这个儿子是死而复活，失而又得的。他们就快乐起来。
chapters.en: For this my son was dead, and is alive again; he was lost, and is found. And they began to be merry.

chapters.zh: 那时，大儿子正在田里。他回来，离家不远，听见作乐跳舞的声音，
chapters.en: Now his elder son was in the field: and as he came and drew nigh to the house, he heard musick and dancing.

chapters.zh: 便叫过一个仆人来，问是甚么事。
chapters.en: And he called one of the servants, and asked what these things meant.

chapters.zh: 仆人说：你兄弟来了；你父亲因为得他无灾无病的回来，把肥牛犊宰了。
chapters.en: And he said unto him, Thy brother is come; and thy father hath killed the fatted calf, because he hath received him safe and sound.

chapters.zh: 大儿子却生气，不肯进去；他父亲就出来劝他。
chapters.en: And he was angry, and would not go in: therefore came his father out, and intreated him.

chapters.zh: 他对父亲说：我服事你这多年，从来没有违背过你的命，你并没有给我一只山羊羔，叫我和朋友一同快乐。
chapters.en: And he answering said to father, Lo, these many years do I serve thee, neither transgressed I at any time thy commandment: and yet thou never gavest me a kid, that I might make merry with my friends:

chapters.zh: 但你这个儿子和娼妓吞尽了你的产业，他一来了，你倒为他宰了肥牛犊。
chapters.en: But as soon as this thy son was come, which hath devoured thy living with harlots, thou hast killed for him the fatted calf.

chapters.zh: 父亲对他说：儿阿！你常和我同在，我一切所有的都是你的；
chapters.en: And he said unto him, Son, thou art ever with me, and all that I have is thine.

chapters.zh: 只是你这个兄弟是死而复活、失而又得的，所以我们理当欢喜快乐。
chapters.en: It was meet that we should make merry, and be glad: for this thy brother was dead, and is alive again; and was lost, and is found.

chapters.zh: 耶稣又对门徒说：有一个财主的管家，别人向他主人告他浪费主人的财物。
chapters.en: And he said also unto his disciples, There was a certain rich man, which had a steward; and the same was accused unto him that he had wasted his goods.

chapters.zh: 主人叫他来，对他说：我听见你这事怎么样呢？把你所经管的交代明白，因你不能再作我的管家。
chapters.en: And he called him, and said unto him, How is it that I hear this of thee? give an account of thy stewardship; for thou mayest be no longer steward.

chapters.zh: 那管家心里说：主人辞我，不用我再作管家，我将来作甚么？锄地呢？无力；讨饭呢？怕羞。
chapters.en: Then the steward said within himself, What shall I do? for my lord taketh away from me the stewardship: I cannot dig; to beg I am ashamed.

chapters.zh: 我知道怎么行，好叫人在我不作管家之后，接我到他们家里去。
chapters.en: I am resolved what to do, that, when I am put out of the stewardship, they may receive me into their houses.

chapters.zh: 于是把欠他主人债的，一个一个的叫了来，问头一个说：你欠我主人多少？
chapters.en: So he called every one of his lord's debtors , and said unto the first, How much owest thou unto my lord?

chapters.zh: 他说：一百篓（每篓约五十斤）油。管家说：拿你的账，快坐下，写五十。
chapters.en: And he said, An hundred measures of oil. And he said unto him, Take thy bill, and sit down quickly, and write fifty.

chapters.zh: 又问一个说：你欠多少？他说：一百石麦子。管家说：拿你的账，写八十。
chapters.en: Then said he to another, And how much owest thou? And he said, An hundred measures of wheat. And he said unto him, Take thy bill, and write fourscore.

chapters.zh: 主人就夸奖这不义的管家做事聪明。因为今世之子，在世事之上，较比光明之子更加聪明。
chapters.en: And the lord commended the unjust steward, because he had done wisely: for the children of this world are in their generation wiser than the children of light.

chapters.zh: 我又告诉你们，要藉著那不义的钱财结交朋友，到了钱财无用的时候，他们可以接你们到永存的帐幕里去。
chapters.en: And I say unto you, Make to yourselves friends of the mammon of unrighteousness; that, when ye fail, they may receive you into everlasting habitations.

chapters.zh: 人在最小的事上忠心，在大事上也忠心；在最小的事上不义，在大事上也不义。
chapters.en: He that is faithful in that which is least is faithful also in much: and he that is unjust in the least is unjust also in much.

chapters.zh: 倘若你们在不义的钱财上不忠心，谁还把那真实的钱财托付你们呢？
chapters.en: If therefore ye have not been faithful in the unrighteous mammon, who will commit to your trust the true ?

chapters.zh: 倘若你们在别人的东西上不忠心，谁还把你们自己的东西给你们呢？
chapters.en: And if ye have not been faithful in that which is another man's, who shall give you that which is your own?

chapters.zh: 一个仆人不能事奉两个主；不是恶这个爱那个，就是重这个轻那个。你们不能又事奉神，又事奉玛门。
chapters.en: No servant can serve two masters: for either he will hate the one, and love the other; or else he will hold to the one, and despise the other. Ye cannot serve God and mammon.

chapters.zh: 法利赛人是贪爱钱财的，他们听见这一切话，就嗤笑耶稣。
chapters.en: And the Pharisees also, who were covetous, heard all these things: and they derided him.

chapters.zh: 耶稣对他们说：你们是在人面前自称为义的，你们的心，神却知道；因为人所尊贵的，是神看为可憎恶的。
chapters.en: And he said unto them, Ye are they which justify yourselves before men; but God knoweth your hearts: for that which is highly esteemed among men is abomination in the sight of God.

chapters.zh: 律法和先知到约翰为止，从此神国的福音传开了，人人努力要进去。
chapters.en: The law and the prophets until John: since that time the kingdom of God is preached, and every man presseth into it.

chapters.zh: 天地废去较比律法的一点一画落空还容易。
chapters.en: And it is easier for heaven and earth to pass, than one tittle of the law to fail.

chapters.zh: 凡休妻另娶的就是犯奸淫；娶被休之妻的也是犯奸淫。
chapters.en: Whosoever putteth away his wife, and marrieth another, committeth adultery: and whosoever marrieth her that is put away from husband committeth adultery.

chapters.zh: 有一个财主穿著紫色袍和细麻布衣服，天天奢华宴乐。
chapters.en: There was a certain rich man, which was clothed in purple and fine linen, and fared sumptuously every day:

chapters.zh: 又有一个讨饭的，名叫拉撒路，浑身生疮，被人放在财主门口，
chapters.en: And there was a certain beggar named Lazarus, which was laid at his gate, full of sores,

chapters.zh: 要得财主桌子上掉下来的零碎充饥；并且狗来餂他的疮。
chapters.en: And desiring to be fed with the crumbs which fell from the rich man's table: moreover the dogs came and licked his sores.

chapters.zh: 后来那讨饭的死了，被天使带去放在亚伯拉罕的怀里。财主也死了，并且埋葬了。
chapters.en: And it came to pass, that the beggar died, and was carried by the angels into Abraham's bosom: the rich man also died, and was buried;

chapters.zh: 他在阴间受痛苦，举目远远的望见亚伯拉罕，又望见拉撒路在他怀里，
chapters.en: And in hell he lift up his eyes, being in torments, and seeth Abraham afar off, and Lazarus in his bosom.

chapters.zh: 就喊著说：我祖亚伯拉罕哪，可怜我罢！打发拉撒路来，用指头尖蘸点水，凉凉我的舌头；因为我在这火燄里，极其痛苦。
chapters.en: And he cried and said, Father Abraham, have mercy on me, and send Lazarus, that he may dip the tip of his finger in water, and cool my tongue; for I am tormented in this flame.

chapters.zh: 亚伯拉罕说：儿阿，你该回想你生前享过福，拉撒路也受过苦；如今他在这里得安慰，你倒受痛苦。
chapters.en: But Abraham said, Son, remember that thou in thy lifetime receivedst thy good things, and likewise Lazarus evil things: but now he is comforted, and thou art tormented.

chapters.zh: 不但这样，并且在你我之间，有深渊限定，以致人要从这边过到你们那边是不能的；要从那边过到我们这边也是不能的。
chapters.en: And beside all this, between us and you there is a great gulf fixed: so that they which would pass from hence to you cannot; neither can they pass to us, that from thence.

chapters.zh: 财主说：我祖阿！既是这样，求你打发拉撒路到我父家去；
chapters.en: Then he said, I pray thee therefore, father, that thou wouldest send him to my father's house:

chapters.zh: 因为我还有五个弟兄，他可以对他们作见证，免得他们也来到这痛苦的地方。
chapters.en: For I have five brethren; that he may testify unto them, lest they also come into this place of torment.

chapters.zh: 亚伯拉罕说：他们有摩西和先知的话可以听从。
chapters.en: Abraham saith unto him, They have Moses and the prophets; let them hear them.

chapters.zh: 他说：我祖亚伯拉罕哪，不是的，若有一个从死里复活的，到他们那里去的，他们必要悔改。
chapters.en: And he said, Nay, father Abraham: but if one went unto them from the dead, they will repent.

chapters.zh: 亚伯拉罕说：若不听从摩西和先知的话，就是有一个从死里复活的，他们也是不听劝。
chapters.en: And he said unto him, If they hear not Moses and the prophets, neither will they be persuaded, though one rose from the dead.

chapters.zh: 耶稣又对门徒说：绊倒人的事是免不了的；但那绊倒人的有祸了。
chapters.en: Then said he unto the disciples, It is impossible but that offences will come: but woe , through whom they come!

chapters.zh: 就是把磨石拴在这人的颈项上，丢在海里，还强如他把这小子里的一个绊倒了。
chapters.en: It were better for him that a millstone were hanged about his neck, and he cast into the sea, than that he should offend one of these little ones.

chapters.zh: 你们要谨慎！若是你的弟兄得罪你，就劝戒他；他若懊悔，就饶恕他。
chapters.en: Take heed to yourselves: If thy brother trespass against thee, rebuke him; and if he repent, forgive him.

chapters.zh: 倘若他一天七次得罪你，又七次回转，说：我懊悔了，你总要饶恕他。
chapters.en: And if he trespass against thee seven times in a day, and seven times in a day turn again to thee, saying, I repent; thou shalt forgive him.

chapters.zh: 使徒对主说：求主加增我们的信心。
chapters.en: And the apostles said unto the Lord, Increase our faith.

chapters.zh: 主说：你们若有信心像一粒芥菜种，就是对这棵桑树说：你要拔起根来，栽在海里，他也必听从你们。
chapters.en: And the Lord said, If ye had faith as a grain of mustard seed, ye might say unto this sycamine tree, Be thou plucked up by the root, and be thou planted in the sea; and it should obey you.

chapters.zh: 你们谁有仆人耕地或是放羊，从田里回来，就对他说：你快来坐下吃饭呢？
chapters.en: But which of you, having a servant plowing or feeding cattle, will say unto him by and by, when he is come from the field, Go and sit down to meat?

chapters.zh: 岂不对他说：你给我预备晚饭，束上带子伺候我，等我吃喝完了，你才可以吃喝么？
chapters.en: And will not rather say unto him, Make ready wherewith I may sup, and gird thyself, and serve me, till I have eaten and drunken; and afterward thou shalt eat and drink?

chapters.zh: 仆人照所吩咐的去做，主人还谢谢他么？
chapters.en: Doth he thank that servant because he did the things that were commanded him? I trow not.

chapters.zh: 这样，你们做完了一切所吩咐的，只当说：我们是无用的仆人，所做的本是我们应分做的。
chapters.en: So likewise ye, when ye shall have done all those things which are commanded you, say, We are unprofitable servants: we have done that which was our duty to do.

chapters.zh: 耶稣往耶路撒冷去，经过撒玛利亚和加利利。
chapters.en: And it came to pass, as he went to Jerusalem, that he passed through the midst of Samaria and Galilee.

chapters.zh: 进入一个村子，有十个长大痲疯的，迎面而来，远远的站著，
chapters.en: And as he entered into a certain village, there met him ten men that were lepers, which stood afar off:

chapters.zh: 高声说：耶稣，夫子，可怜我们罢！
chapters.en: And they lifted up voices, and said, Jesus, Master, have mercy on us.

chapters.zh: 耶稣看见，就对他们说：你们去把身体给祭司察看。他们去的时候就洁净了。
chapters.en: And when he saw , he said unto them, Go shew yourselves unto the priests. And it came to pass, that, as they went, they were cleansed.

chapters.zh: 内中有一个见自己已经好了，就回来大声归荣耀与神，
chapters.en: And one of them, when he saw that he was healed, turned back, and with a loud voice glorified God,

chapters.zh: 又俯伏在耶稣脚前感谢他；这人是撒玛利亚人。
chapters.en: And fell down on face at his feet, giving him thanks: and he was a Samaritan.

chapters.zh: 耶稣说：洁净了的不是十个人么？那九个在那里呢？
chapters.en: And Jesus answering said, Were there not ten cleansed? but where the nine?

chapters.zh: 除了这外族人，再没有别人回来归荣耀与神么？
chapters.en: There are not found that returned to give glory to God, save this stranger.

chapters.zh: 就对那人说：起来，走罢！你的信救了你了。
chapters.en: And he said unto him, Arise, go thy way: thy faith hath made thee whole.

chapters.zh: 法利赛人问：神的国几时来到？耶稣回答说：神的国来到不是眼所能见的。
chapters.en: And when he was demanded of the Pharisees, when the kingdom of God should come, he answered them and said, The kingdom of God cometh not with observation:

chapters.zh: 人也不得说：看哪，在这里！看哪，在那里！因为神的国就在你们心里（心里：或作中间）。
chapters.en: Neither shall they say, Lo here! or, lo there! for, behold, the kingdom of God is within you.

chapters.zh: 他又对门徒说：日子将到，你们巴不得看见人子的一个日子，却不得看见。
chapters.en: And he said unto the disciples, The days will come, when ye shall desire to see one of the days of the Son of man, and ye shall not see .

chapters.zh: 人将要对你们说：看哪，在那里！看哪，在这里！你们不要出去，也不要跟随他们！
chapters.en: And they shall say to you, See here; or, see there: go not after , nor follow .

chapters.zh: 因为人子在他降临的日子，好像闪电从天这边一闪直照到天那边。
chapters.en: For as the lightning, that lighteneth out of the one under heaven, shineth unto the other under heaven; so shall also the Son of man be in his day.

chapters.zh: 只是他必须先受许多苦，又被这世代弃绝。
chapters.en: But first must he suffer many things, and be rejected of this generation.

chapters.zh: 挪亚的日子怎样，人子的日子也要怎样。
chapters.en: And as it was in the days of Noe, so shall it be also in the days of the Son of man.

chapters.zh: 那时候的人又吃又喝，又娶又嫁，到挪亚进方舟的那日，洪水就来，把他们全都灭了。
chapters.en: They did eat, they drank, they married wives, they were given in marriage, until the day that Noe entered into the ark, and the flood came, and destroyed them all.

chapters.zh: 又好像罗得的日子；人又吃又喝，又买又卖，又耕种又盖造。
chapters.en: Likewise also as it was in the days of Lot; they did eat, they drank, they bought, they sold, they planted, they builded;

chapters.zh: 到罗得出所多玛的那日，就有火与硫磺从天上降下来，把他们全都灭了。
chapters.en: But the same day that Lot went out of Sodom it rained fire and brimstone from heaven, and destroyed all.

chapters.zh: 人子显现的日子也要这样。
chapters.en: Even thus shall it be in the day when the Son of man is revealed.

chapters.zh: 当那日，人在房上，器具在屋里，不要下来拿；人在田里，也不要回家。
chapters.en: In that day, he which shall be upon the housetop, and his stuff in the house, let him not come down to take it away: and he that is in the field, let him likewise not return back.

chapters.zh: 你们要回想罗得的妻子。
chapters.en: Remember Lot's wife.

chapters.zh: 凡想要保全生命的，必丧掉生命；凡丧掉生命的，必救活生命。
chapters.en: Whosoever shall seek to save his life shall lose it; and whosoever shall lose his life shall preserve it.

chapters.zh: 我对你们说，当那一夜，两个人在一个床上，要取去一个，撇下一个。
chapters.en: I tell you, in that night there shall be two in one bed; the one shall be taken, and the other shall be left.

chapters.zh: 两个女人一同推磨；要取去一个，撇下一个。（有古卷在此有：
chapters.en: Two shall be grinding together; the one shall be taken, and the other left.

chapters.zh: 两个人在田里，要取去一个，撇下一个。）
chapters.en: Two shall be in the field; the one shall be taken, and the other left.

chapters.zh: 门徒说：主阿，在那里有这事呢？耶稣说：尸首在那里，鹰也必聚在那里。
chapters.en: And they answered and said unto him, Where, Lord? And he said unto them, Wheresoever the body , thither will the eagles be gathered together.

chapters.zh: 耶稣设一个比喻，是要人常常祷告，不可灰心。
chapters.en: And he spake a parable unto them , that men ought always to pray, and not to faint;

chapters.zh: 说：某城里有一个官，不惧怕神，也不尊重世人。
chapters.en: Saying, There was in a city a judge, which feared not God, neither regarded man:

chapters.zh: 那城里有个寡妇，常到他那里，说：我有一个对头，求你给我伸冤。
chapters.en: And there was a widow in that city; and she came unto him, saying, Avenge me of mine adversary.

chapters.zh: 他多日不准。后来心里说：我虽不惧怕神，也不尊重世人，
chapters.en: And he would not for a while: but afterward he said within himself, Though I fear not God, nor regard man;

chapters.zh: 只因这寡妇烦扰我，我就给他伸冤罢，免得他常来缠磨我！
chapters.en: Yet because this widow troubleth me, I will avenge her, lest by her continual coming she weary me.

chapters.zh: 主说：你们听这不义之官所说的话。
chapters.en: And the Lord said, Hear what the unjust judge saith.

chapters.zh: 神的选民昼夜呼吁他，他纵然为他们忍了多时，岂不终久给他们伸冤么？
chapters.en: And shall not God avenge his own elect, which cry day and night unto him, though he bear long with them?

chapters.zh: 我告诉你们，要快快的给他们伸冤了。然而，人子来的时候，遇得见世上有信德么？
chapters.en: I tell you that he will avenge them speedily. Nevertheless when the Son of man cometh, shall he find faith on the earth?

chapters.zh: 耶稣向那些仗著自己是义人，藐视别人的，设一个比喻，
chapters.en: And he spake this parable unto certain which trusted in themselves that they were righteous, and despised others:

chapters.zh: 说：有两个人上殿里去祷告：一个是法利赛人，一个是税吏。
chapters.en: Two men went up into the temple to pray; the one a Pharisee, and the other a publican.

chapters.zh: 法利赛人站著，自言自语的祷告说：神阿，我感谢你，我不像别人勒索、不义、奸淫，也不像这个税吏。
chapters.en: The Pharisee stood and prayed thus with himself, God, I thank thee, that I am not as other men , extortioners, unjust, adulterers, or even as this publican.

chapters.zh: 我一个礼拜禁食两次，凡我所得的都捐上十分之一。
chapters.en: I fast twice in the week, I give tithes of all that I possess.

chapters.zh: 那税吏远远的站著，连举目望天也不敢，只捶著胸说：神阿，开恩可怜我这个罪人！
chapters.en: And the publican, standing afar off, would not lift up so much as eyes unto heaven, but smote upon his breast, saying, God be merciful to me a sinner.

chapters.zh: 我告诉你们，这人回家去比那人倒算为义了；因为，凡自高的，必降为卑；自卑的，必升为高。
chapters.en: I tell you, this man went down to his house justified than the other: for every one that exalteth himself shall be abased; and he that humbleth himself shall be exalted.

chapters.zh: 有人抱著自己的婴孩来见耶稣，要他摸他们；门徒看见就责备那些人。
chapters.en: And they brought unto him also infants, that he would touch them: but when disciples saw , they rebuked them.

chapters.zh: 耶稣却叫他们来，说：让小孩子到我这里来，不要禁止他们；因为在神国的正是这样的人。
chapters.en: But Jesus called them , and said, Suffer little children to come unto me, and forbid them not: for of such is the kingdom of God.

chapters.zh: 我实在告诉你们，凡要承受神国的，若不像小孩子，断不能进去。
chapters.en: Verily I say unto you, Whosoever shall not receive the kingdom of God as a little child shall in no wise enter therein.

chapters.zh: 有一个官问耶稣说：良善的夫子，我该做甚么事才可以承受永生？
chapters.en: And a certain ruler asked him, saying, Good Master, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?

chapters.zh: 耶稣对他说：你为甚么称我是良善的？除了神一位之外，再没有良善的。
chapters.en: And Jesus said unto him, Why callest thou me good? none good, save one, , God.

chapters.zh: 诫命你是晓得的：不可奸淫；不可杀人；不可偷盗；不可作假见证；当孝敬父母。
chapters.en: Thou knowest the commandments, Do not commit adultery, Do not kill, Do not steal, Do not bear false witness, Honour thy father and thy mother.

chapters.zh: 那人说：这一切我从小都遵守了。
chapters.en: And he said, All these have I kept from my youth up.

chapters.zh: 耶稣听见了，就说：你还缺少一件：要变卖你一切所有的，分给穷人，就必有财宝在天上；你还要来跟从我。
chapters.en: Now when Jesus heard these things, he said unto him, Yet lackest thou one thing: sell all that thou hast, and distribute unto the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven: and come, follow me.

chapters.zh: 他听见这话，就甚忧愁，因为他很富足。
chapters.en: And when he heard this, he was very sorrowful: for he was very rich.

chapters.zh: 耶稣看见他，就说：有钱财的人进神的国是何等的难哪！
chapters.en: And when Jesus saw that he was very sorrowful, he said, How hardly shall they that have riches enter into the kingdom of God!

chapters.zh: 骆驼穿过针的眼比财主进神的国还容易呢！
chapters.en: For it is easier for a camel to go through a needle's eye, than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.

chapters.zh: 听见的人说：这样，谁能得救呢？
chapters.en: And they that heard said, Who then can be saved?

chapters.zh: 耶稣说：在人所不能的事，在神却能。
chapters.en: And he said, The things which are impossible with men are possible with God.

chapters.zh: 彼得说：看哪，我们已经撇下自己所有的跟从你了。
chapters.en: Then Peter said, Lo, we have left all, and followed thee.

chapters.zh: 耶稣说：我实在告诉你们，人为神的国撇下房屋，或是妻子、弟兄、父母、儿女，
chapters.en: And he said unto them, Verily I say unto you, There is no man that hath left house, or parents, or brethren, or wife, or children, for the kingdom of God's sake,

chapters.zh: 没有在今世不得百倍，在来世不得永生的。
chapters.en: Who shall not receive manifold more in this present time, and in the world to come life everlasting.

chapters.zh: 耶稣带著十二个门徒，对他们说：看哪，我们上耶路撒冷去，先知所写的一切事都要成就在人子身上。
chapters.en: Then he took the twelve, and said unto them, Behold, we go up to Jerusalem, and all things that are written by the prophets concerning the Son of man shall be accomplished.

chapters.zh: 他将要被交给外邦人，他们要戏弄他，凌辱他，吐唾沫在他脸上，
chapters.en: For he shall be delivered unto the Gentiles, and shall be mocked, and spitefully entreated, and spitted on:

chapters.zh: 并要鞭打他，杀害他；第三日他要复活。
chapters.en: And they shall scourge , and put him to death: and the third day he shall rise again.

chapters.zh: 这些事门徒一样也不懂得，意思乃是隐藏的；他们不晓得所说的是甚么。
chapters.en: And they understood none of these things: and this saying was hid from them, neither knew they the things which were spoken.

chapters.zh: 耶稣将近耶利哥的时候，有一个瞎子坐在路旁讨饭。
chapters.en: And it came to pass, that as he was come nigh unto Jericho, a certain blind man sat by the way side begging:

chapters.zh: 听见许多人经过，就问是甚么事。
chapters.en: And hearing the multitude pass by, he asked what it meant.

chapters.zh: 他们告诉他，是拿撒勒人耶稣经过。
chapters.en: And they told him, that Jesus of Nazareth passeth by.

chapters.zh: 他就呼叫说：大卫的子孙耶稣阿，可怜我罢！
chapters.en: And he cried, saying, Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me.

chapters.zh: 在前头走的人就责备他，不许他作声；他却越发喊叫说：大卫的子孙，可怜我罢！
chapters.en: And they which went before rebuked him, that he should hold his peace: but he cried so much the more, Son of David, have mercy on me.

chapters.zh: 耶稣站住，吩咐把他领过来，到了跟前，就问他说：
chapters.en: And Jesus stood, and commanded him to be brought unto him: and when he was come near, he asked him,

chapters.zh: 你要我为你做甚么？他说：主阿，我要能看见。
chapters.en: Saying, What wilt thou that I shall do unto thee? And he said, Lord, that I may receive my sight.

chapters.zh: 耶稣说：你可以看见！你的信救了你了。
chapters.en: And Jesus said unto him, Receive thy sight: thy faith hath saved thee.

chapters.zh: 瞎子立刻看见了，就跟随耶稣，一路归荣耀与神。众人看见这事，也赞美神。
chapters.en: And immediately he received his sight, and followed him, glorifying God: and all the people, when they saw , gave praise unto God.

chapters.zh: 耶稣进了耶利哥，正经过的时候，
chapters.en: And entered and passed through Jericho.

chapters.zh: 有一个人名叫撒该，作税吏长，是个财主。
chapters.en: And, behold, a man named Zacchaeus, which was the chief among the publicans, and he was rich.

chapters.zh: 他要看看耶稣是怎样的人；只因人多，他的身量又矮，所以不得看见，
chapters.en: And he sought to see Jesus who he was; and could not for the press, because he was little of stature.

chapters.zh: 就跑到前头，爬上桑树，要看耶稣，因为耶稣必从那里经过。
chapters.en: And he ran before, and climbed up into a sycomore tree to see him: for he was to pass that .

chapters.zh: 耶稣到了那里，抬头一看，对他说：撒该，快下来！今天我必住在你家里。
chapters.en: And when Jesus came to the place, he looked up, and saw him, and said unto him, Zacchaeus, make haste, and come down; for to day I must abide at thy house.

chapters.zh: 他就急忙下来，欢欢喜喜的接待耶稣。
chapters.en: And he made haste, and came down, and received him joyfully.

chapters.zh: 众人看见，都私下议论说：他竟到罪人家里去住宿。
chapters.en: And when they saw , they all murmured, saying, That he was gone to be guest with a man that is a sinner.

chapters.zh: 撒该站著对主说：主阿，我把所有的一半给穷人；我若讹诈了谁，就还他四倍。
chapters.en: And Zacchaeus stood, and said unto the Lord; Behold, Lord, the half of my goods I give to the poor; and if I have taken any thing from any man by false accusation, I restore fourfold.

chapters.zh: 耶稣说：今天救恩到了这家，因为他也是亚伯拉罕的子孙。
chapters.en: And Jesus said unto him, This day is salvation come to this house, forsomuch as he also is a son of Abraham.

chapters.zh: 人子来，为要寻找，拯救失丧的人。
chapters.en: For the Son of man is come to seek and to save that which was lost.

chapters.zh: 众人正在听见这些话的时候，耶稣因为将近耶路撒冷，又因他们以为神的国快要显出来，就另设一个比喻，说：
chapters.en: And as they heard these things, he added and spake a parable, because he was nigh to Jerusalem, and because they thought that the kingdom of God should immediately appear.

chapters.zh: 有一个贵胄往远方去，要得国回来，
chapters.en: He said therefore, A certain nobleman went into a far country to receive for himself a kingdom, and to return.

chapters.zh: 便叫了他的十个仆人来，交给他们十锭（锭：原文作弥拿约银十两）银子，说：你们去作生意，直等我回来。
chapters.en: And he called his ten servants, and delivered them ten pounds, and said unto them, Occupy till I come.

chapters.zh: 他本国的人却恨他，打发使者随后去，说：我们不愿意这个人作我们的王。
chapters.en: But his citizens hated him, and sent a message after him, saying, We will not have this to reign over us.

chapters.zh: 他既得国回来，就吩咐叫那领银子的仆人来，要知道他们做生意赚了多少。
chapters.en: And it came to pass, that when he was returned, having received the kingdom, then he commanded these servants to be called unto him, to whom he had given the money, that he might know how much every man had gained by trading.

chapters.zh: 头一个上来，说：主阿，你的一锭银子已经赚了十锭。
chapters.en: Then came the first, saying, Lord, thy pound hath gained ten pounds.

chapters.zh: 主人说：好！良善的仆人，你既在最小的事上有忠心，可以有权柄管十座城。
chapters.en: And he said unto him, Well, thou good servant: because thou hast been faithful in a very little, have thou authority over ten cities.

chapters.zh: 第二个来，说：主阿！你的一锭银子，已经赚了五锭。
chapters.en: And the second came, saying, Lord, thy pound hath gained five pounds.

chapters.zh: 主人说：你也可以管五座城。
chapters.en: And he said likewise to him, Be thou also over five cities.

chapters.zh: 又有一个来说：主阿，看哪，你的一锭银子在这里，我把他包在手巾里存著。
chapters.en: And another came, saying, Lord, behold, thy pound, which I have kept laid up in a napkin:

chapters.zh: 我原是怕你，因为你是严厉的人；没有放下的，还要去拿；没有种下的，还要去收。
chapters.en: For I feared thee, because thou art an austere man: thou takest up that thou layedst not down, and reapest that thou didst not sow.

chapters.zh: 主人对他说：你这恶仆，我要凭你的口定你的罪。你既知道我是严厉的人，没有放下的，还要去拿，没有种下的，还要去收，
chapters.en: And he saith unto him, Out of thine own mouth will I judge thee, wicked servant. Thou knewest that I was an austere man, taking up that I laid not down, and reaping that I did not sow:

chapters.zh: 为甚么不把我的银子交给银行，等我来的时候，连本带利都可以要回来呢？
chapters.en: Wherefore then gavest not thou my money into the bank, that at my coming I might have required mine own with usury?

chapters.zh: 就对旁边站著的人说：夺过他这一锭来，给那有十锭的。
chapters.en: And he said unto them that stood by, Take from him the pound, and give to him that hath ten pounds.

chapters.zh: 他们说：主阿，他已经有十锭了。
chapters.en: (And they said unto him, Lord, he hath ten pounds.)

chapters.zh: 主人说：我告诉你们，凡有的，还要加给他；没有的，连他所有的也要夺过来。
chapters.en: For I say unto you, That unto every one which hath shall be given; and from him that hath not, even that he hath shall be taken away from him.

chapters.zh: 至于我那些仇敌，不要我作他们王的，把他们拉来，在我面前杀了罢！
chapters.en: But those mine enemies, which would not that I should reign over them, bring hither, and slay before me.

chapters.zh: 耶稣说完了这话，就在前面走，上耶路撒冷去。
chapters.en: And when he had thus spoken, he went before, ascending up to Jerusalem.

chapters.zh: 将近伯法其和伯大尼，在一座山名叫橄榄山那里，就打发两个门徒，说：
chapters.en: And it came to pass, when he was come nigh to Bethphage and Bethany, at the mount called of Olives, he sent two of his disciples,

chapters.zh: 你们往对面村子里去，进去的时候，必看见一匹驴驹拴在那里，是从来没有人骑过的，可以解开牵来。
chapters.en: Saying, Go ye into the village over against ; in the which at your entering ye shall find a colt tied, whereon yet never man sat: loose him, and bring .

chapters.zh: 若有人问为甚么解他，你们就说：主要用他。
chapters.en: And if any man ask you, Why do ye loose ? thus shall ye say unto him, Because the Lord hath need of him.

chapters.zh: 打发的人去了，所遇见的正如耶稣所说的。
chapters.en: And they that were sent went their way, and found even as he had said unto them.

chapters.zh: 他们解驴驹的时候，主人问他们说：解驴驹做甚么？
chapters.en: And as they were loosing the colt, the owners thereof said unto them, Why loose ye the colt?

chapters.zh: 他们说：主要用他。
chapters.en: And they said, The Lord hath need of him.

chapters.zh: 他们牵到耶稣那里，把自己的衣服搭在上面，扶著耶稣骑上。
chapters.en: And they brought him to Jesus: and they cast their garments upon the colt, and they set Jesus thereon.

chapters.zh: 走的时候，众人把衣服铺在路上。
chapters.en: And as he went, they spread their clothes in the way.

chapters.zh: 将近耶路撒冷，正下橄榄山的时候，众门徒因所见过的一切异能，都欢乐起来，大声赞美神，
chapters.en: And when he was come nigh, even now at the descent of the mount of Olives, the whole multitude of the disciples began to rejoice and praise God with a loud voice for all the mighty works that they had seen;

chapters.zh: 说：奉主名来的王是应当称颂的！在天上有和平；在至高之处有荣光。
chapters.en: Saying, Blessed the King that cometh in the name of the Lord: peace in heaven, and glory in the highest.

chapters.zh: 众人中有几个法利赛人对耶稣说：夫子，责备你的门徒罢！
chapters.en: And some of the Pharisees from among the multitude said unto him, Master, rebuke thy disciples.

chapters.zh: 耶稣说：我告诉你们，若是他们闭口不说，这些石头必要呼叫起来。
chapters.en: And he answered and said unto them, I tell you that, if these should hold their peace, the stones would immediately cry out.

chapters.zh: 耶稣快到耶路撒冷，看见城，就为他哀哭，
chapters.en: And when he was come near, he beheld the city, and wept over it,

chapters.zh: 说：巴不得你在这日子知道关系你平安的事；无奈这事现在是隐藏的，叫你的眼看不出来。
chapters.en: Saying, If thou hadst known, even thou, at least in this thy day, the things unto thy peace! but now they are hid from thine eyes.

chapters.zh: 因为日子将到，你的仇敌必筑起土垒，周围环绕你，四面困住你，
chapters.en: For the days shall come upon thee, that thine enemies shall cast a trench about thee, and compass thee round, and keep thee in on every side,

chapters.zh: 并要扫灭你和你里头的儿女，连一块石头也不留在石头上，因你不知道眷顾你的时候。
chapters.en: And shall lay thee even with the ground, and thy children within thee; and they shall not leave in thee one stone upon another; because thou knewest not the time of thy visitation.

chapters.zh: 耶稣进了殿，赶出里头做买卖的人，
chapters.en: And he went into the temple, and began to cast out them that sold therein, and them that bought;

chapters.zh: 对他们说：经上说：我的殿必作祷告的殿，你们倒使他成为贼窝了。
chapters.en: Saying unto them, It is written, My house is the house of prayer: but ye have made it a den of thieves.

chapters.zh: 耶稣天天在殿里教训人。祭司长和文士与百姓的尊长都想要杀他，
chapters.en: And he taught daily in the temple. But the chief priests and the scribes and the chief of the people sought to destroy him,

chapters.zh: 但寻不出法子来，因为百姓都侧耳听他。
chapters.en: And could not find what they might do: for all the people were very attentive to hear him.

chapters.zh: 有一天，耶稣在殿里教训百姓，讲福音的时候，祭司长和文士并长老上前来，
chapters.en: And it came to pass, on one of those days, as he taught the people in the temple, and preached the gospel, the chief priests and the scribes came upon with the elders,

chapters.zh: 问他说：你告诉我们，你仗著甚么权柄做这些事？给你这权柄的是谁呢？
chapters.en: And spake unto him, saying, Tell us, by what authority doest thou these things? or who is he that gave thee this authority?

chapters.zh: 耶稣回答说：我也要问你们一句话，你们且告诉我。
chapters.en: And he answered and said unto them, I will also ask you one thing; and answer me:

chapters.zh: 约翰的洗礼是从天上来的？是从人间来的呢？
chapters.en: The baptism of John, was it from heaven, or of men?

chapters.zh: 他们彼此商议说：我们若说从天上来，他必说：你们为甚么不信他呢？
chapters.en: And they reasoned with themselves, saying, If we shall say, From heaven; he will say, Why then believed ye him not?

chapters.zh: 若说从人间来，百姓都要用石头打死我们，因为他们信约翰是先知。
chapters.en: But and if we say, Of men; all the people will stone us: for they be persuaded that John was a prophet.

chapters.zh: 于是回答说：不知道是从那里来的。
chapters.en: And they answered, that they could not tell whence .

chapters.zh: 耶稣说：我也不告诉你们，我仗著甚么权柄做这些事。
chapters.en: And Jesus said unto them, Neither tell I you by what authority I do these things.

chapters.zh: 耶稣就设比喻对百姓说：有人栽了一个葡萄园，租给园户，就往外国去住了许久。
chapters.en: Then began he to speak to the people this parable; A certain man planted a vineyard, and let it forth to husbandmen, and went into a far country for a long time.

chapters.zh: 到了时候，打发一个仆人到园户那里去，叫他们把园中当纳的果子交给他；园户竟打了他，叫他空手回去。
chapters.en: And at the season he sent a servant to the husbandmen, that they should give him of the fruit of the vineyard: but the husbandmen beat him, and sent away empty.

chapters.zh: 又打发一个仆人去，他们也打了他，并且凌辱他，叫他空手回去。
chapters.en: And again he sent another servant: and they beat him also, and entreated shamefully, and sent away empty.

chapters.zh: 又打发第三个仆人去，他们也打伤了他，把他推出去了。
chapters.en: And again he sent a third: and they wounded him also, and cast out.

chapters.zh: 园主说：我怎么办呢？我要打发我的爱子去，或者他们尊敬他。
chapters.en: Then said the lord of the vineyard, What shall I do? I will send my beloved son: it may be they will reverence when they see him.

chapters.zh: 不料，园户看见他，就彼此商量说：这是承受产业的，我们杀他罢，使产业归于我们！
chapters.en: But when the husbandmen saw him, they reasoned among themselves, saying, This is the heir: come, let us kill him, that the inheritance may be ours.

chapters.zh: 于是把他推出葡萄园外，杀了。这样，葡萄园的主人要怎样处治他们呢？
chapters.en: So they cast him out of the vineyard, and killed . What therefore shall the lord of the vineyard do unto them?

chapters.zh: 他要来除灭这些园户，将葡萄园转给别人。听见的人说：这是万不可的！
